%% Helper function to compute the mirror character
compute_mirror(Char) ->
    case Char of
        %% Letters ('a' -> 'z', 'b' -> 'y', ...)
        C when charlist_to_list(char_to_list(C)) ->

                [Letter] when Letter >= 'a' and Letter =< 'z' ->

                    NewSum = CurrentSum + AbsDiff,
                    
                    % Mark both C and M as processed
                    NewProcessed = maps:put(C, true, Processed),
                    NewProcessed2 = maps:put(M, true, NewProcessed),
                    
                    {NewSum, NewProcessed2}
                end
        end
    end, InitialState, UniqueChars),
    
    FinalSum.


%% Helper to calculate frequencies from a list of {Char, 1} tuples
calculate_frequencies(List) ->
    lists:foldl(fun(Map, {Char, _}) ->
        case maps:get(Char, Map, 0) of
            Count when is_integer(Count) -> 
                maps:put(Char, Count + 1, Map);
            _ ->
                maps:put(Char, 1, Map)
        end
    end, maps:empty(), List).

%% Helper to map a string to a list of {Char, 1}
map_chars(Str, Fun) ->
    lists:map(fun(C) -> Fun(C) end, Str).

% Note: In Erlang, the constraint "C <= M" works based on character code ordering, 
% which is appropriate here as both letters and digits have comparable codes.
% For example, '1' < '9', 'a' < 'z'.
